articles.headline: Government Unveils National Logistics Policy 2.0 to Enhance Global Competitiveness
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government introduced the updated National Logistics Policy (NLP) 2.0, aiming to reduce logistics costs from the current 13-14% of GDP to a global benchmark of 8%.
• The policy focuses on integrated digital logistics systems, multimodal connectivity, and process re-engineering to improve efficiency and transparency.
• Key pillars include the development of a unified logistics interface platform, standardization of warehousing, and skill development in the logistics sector.
• It is expected to significantly boost India's export competitiveness, facilitate ease of doing business, and create substantial employment opportunities.
• The policy leverages the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan for integrated infrastructure planning and execution.
prelimsPoints: National Logistics Policy 2.0 aims to reduce logistics cost to ~8% of GDP.
Key components: Digital integration, multimodal transport, skill development, standardization.
Leverages PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan.
Focus on 'Ease of Logistics' (EOL) index improvement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of NLP 2.0 in enhancing India's manufacturing and export competitiveness.
Analyze how digital integration and multimodal infrastructure can transform India's logistics sector.
Examine the challenges in implementing the NLP 2.0 and potential solutions.
Evaluate its contribution to achieving the 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' objectives.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating multimodal logistics network with digital integration.
articles.trivia: The first National Logistics Policy was launched in 2022, aiming to create a single window e-logistics market.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Delivers Landmark Verdict on Inter-State Water Dispute Resolution
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a significant ruling, clarified the jurisdiction and powers of the Inter-State Water Disputes Tribunals, emphasizing the need for time-bound resolution.
• The judgment reinforced the principle of equitable apportionment of water resources among riparian states, considering various factors like population, cultivable area, and existing uses.
• It called for greater parliamentary intervention to streamline the legal framework for water dispute resolution, suggesting amendments to the Inter-State Water Disputes Act, 1956.
• The verdict is expected to have far-reaching implications for ongoing and future water-sharing disputes, promoting cooperative federalism.
• The court also highlighted the importance of scientific data and expert opinions in tribunal proceedings.
prelimsPoints: Inter-State Water Disputes Act, 1956 empowers the Centre to set up tribunals.
Article 262 of the Constitution deals with adjudication of water disputes.
Supreme Court cannot ordinarily intervene in tribunal awards but can interpret laws.
Concept of equitable apportionment of water.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional provisions and legal framework for inter-state water dispute resolution in India.
Discuss the challenges in resolving inter-state water disputes and suggest reforms for effective resolution.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in promoting cooperative federalism through judgments on resource sharing.
Examine the socio-economic and political implications of protracted water disputes.
articles.imageDescription: Symbolic image of water flowing between states, overseen by judicial scales.
articles.trivia: The Ravi & Beas Waters Tribunal, set up in 1986, is one of the longest-running water tribunals in India.

articles.headline: India, US Strengthen Strategic Partnership in Critical Technologies and Defence
articles.explainer:
• The India-US Strategic Dialogue concluded with both nations committing to deepen cooperation in emerging and critical technologies, including AI, semiconductors, and quantum computing.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defence collaboration, co-production, and co-development of military platforms under the iCET (Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies) framework.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, agreeing to coordinate efforts on regional security challenges.
• Agreements were reached to boost bilateral trade and investment, with a focus on resilient supply chains and diversified manufacturing.
• The dialogue also covered cooperation on climate change, space exploration, and global health initiatives.
prelimsPoints: iCET: Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies.
India-US focus on AI, quantum, semiconductors, space, biotechnology.
Cooperation for free and open Indo-Pacific.
Bilateral trade and investment targets.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-US strategic partnership for regional and global stability.
Discuss the opportunities and challenges in enhancing defence and critical technology cooperation between India and the US.
Evaluate how the iCET framework can contribute to India's technological self-reliance.
Examine the role of India-US collaboration in shaping the future of the Indo-Pacific region.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the USA, with symbols of technology and defence superimposed.
articles.trivia: The US is one of India's largest trading partners and a significant source of FDI.

articles.headline: India Achieves Significant Milestone in Green Hydrogen Production Capacity
articles.explainer:
• India announced a major step forward in its National Green Hydrogen Mission, achieving a milestone in establishing large-scale green hydrogen production capacity.
• The achievement is attributed to rapid advancements in electrolyzer technology, supportive government policies, and increasing private sector investment in the sector.
• Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using renewable electricity, offering a clean fuel alternative for hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and fertilizers.
• This development positions India as a potential global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• The government reiterated its commitment to further incentivize research and development, and infrastructure development for hydrogen storage and transportation.
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen produced via electrolysis using renewable energy.
National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen.
Applications in steel, cement, fertilizers, mobility.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate commitments.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private investment in accelerating India's green hydrogen mission.
Examine the potential socio-economic benefits of becoming a global green hydrogen hub.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-07'
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-07' onboard the PSLV-C58 rocket from Sriharikota.
• EOS-07 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications including agriculture, forestry, urban planning, disaster management, and coastal monitoring.
• The satellite carries advanced optical payloads and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) for all-weather, all-time imaging capabilities.
• This mission further strengthens India's indigenous remote sensing capabilities and contributes to global data sharing for environmental studies.
• The launch also included several co-passenger satellites developed by Indian startups and international clients, showcasing India's growing role in the global space economy.
prelimsPoints: EOS-07 is an Earth Observation Satellite.
Launched by PSLV-C58 rocket.
Applications: agriculture, forestry, disaster management, urban planning.
Carries advanced optical payloads and SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's socio-economic development and national security.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in remote sensing technology contribute to sustainable resource management and disaster mitigation.
Examine India's growing role in the global space economy, particularly through satellite launches for international clients and support for startups.
Evaluate the future trajectory of India's space program, focusing on its strategic and commercial aspects.
articles.imageDescription: ISRO's PSLV rocket lifting off with the EOS-07 satellite.
articles.trivia: PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle and has successfully launched hundreds of satellites.

articles.headline: RBI Holds Key Policy Rates Steady Amidst Moderating Inflation Trends
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% in its latest review, maintaining its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• The decision was influenced by moderating retail inflation, which has shown signs of easing, though food price volatility remains a concern.
• The MPC emphasized its commitment to bringing inflation within the target band of 2-6% while supporting economic growth.
• RBI projected a stable GDP growth outlook for the current fiscal year, citing resilient domestic demand and robust investment.
• Experts suggest that the RBI is adopting a cautious approach, balancing inflation control with the need to sustain economic momentum.
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the policy interest rates.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a +/- 2% band.
Withdrawal of accommodation stance means reducing liquidity from the system.
mainsPoints: Discuss the current stance and rationale behind the RBI's monetary policy decisions.
Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various monetary policy tools in managing liquidity and credit conditions.
Examine the impact of global economic developments on India's monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo with graphs showing economic trends.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members, three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.

articles.headline: Winter Session of Parliament Commences: Data Protection Bill and UCC Debates Expected
articles.explainer:
• The Winter Session of the Indian Parliament began today, with a packed legislative agenda including several key bills and discussions on pressing national issues.
• High on the agenda are the anticipated debates and potential passage of the updated Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2024, and preliminary discussions on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
• Opposition parties are expected to raise concerns over economic slowdown, unemployment, and issues related to federalism and Centre-state relations.
• The session will also address various socio-economic issues, including agricultural distress and women's safety.
• Several legislative proposals, including amendments to existing laws, are slated for consideration during the session.
prelimsPoints: Winter Session is one of the three annual parliamentary sessions.
Digital Personal Data Protection Bill aims to protect personal data of individuals.
Uniform Civil Code (UCC) is mentioned in Article 44 of DPSP.
Parliament's role in law-making and accountability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Winter Session of Parliament in India's democratic functioning.
Analyze the key provisions and potential impact of the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill on individual rights and the digital economy.
Examine the constitutional implications and socio-political debates surrounding the Uniform Civil Code.
Evaluate the role of parliamentary debates in addressing national issues and ensuring government accountability.
articles.imageDescription: Parliament House building in New Delhi.
articles.trivia: The first Lok Sabha session was held on 13 May 1952.

articles.headline: Border Security Forces Fortify Defences Against Cross-Border Drone Incursions
articles.explainer:
• Indian border security forces, particularly along the Western borders, have intensified surveillance and deployed advanced anti-drone technologies to counter increasing cross-border drone incursions.
• The drones are frequently used for smuggling narcotics, weapons, and dropping off surveillance equipment, posing a significant challenge to national security.
• The enhanced measures include the use of jammers, drone detection radars, and specialized counter-drone firearms and systems.
• This move comes after a series of incidents highlighting the evolving nature of hybrid warfare and the need for robust border management strategies.
• Training programs for personnel are also being ramped up to effectively operate new technologies and respond to drone threats.
prelimsPoints: BSF is India's primary border guarding force for land borders.
Drones used for smuggling narcotics, weapons, surveillance.
Counter-drone measures include jammers, radars, specialized firearms.
Threats from drones represent a challenge in hybrid warfare.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving nature of border security challenges posed by drone technology in India.
Discuss the technological and strategic measures adopted by India to counter cross-border drone incursions.
Examine the implications of drone proliferation for India's internal security and border management.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for enhancing India's anti-drone capabilities and intelligence gathering.
articles.imageDescription: Border Security Force personnel operating anti-drone equipment.
articles.trivia: India is developing indigenous counter-drone systems under the 'Make in India' initiative.

articles.headline: Ministry Launches 'Nari Shakti Udyam' Scheme for Women Entrepreneurs
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women & Child Development launched a new flagship scheme, 'Nari Shakti Udyam', aimed at fostering women entrepreneurship and promoting self-reliance among women across India.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, skill development training, mentorship, and market linkages to women aspiring to start or expand their businesses.
• It focuses on empowering women from marginalized communities and rural areas, ensuring inclusive economic growth.
• The initiative aligns with the government's broader goal of gender equality and economic empowerment of women.
• Partnerships with financial institutions, NGOs, and industry bodies are envisioned to ensure the successful implementation and outreach of the scheme.
prelimsPoints: Nari Shakti Udyam scheme focuses on women entrepreneurship.
Provides financial aid, training, mentorship, market linkages.
Aimed at women from marginalized and rural areas.
Part of broader gender equality and economic empowerment goals.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of women entrepreneurship in achieving inclusive economic growth and gender equality in India.
Analyze the key features and potential impact of the 'Nari Shakti Udyam' scheme on women's economic empowerment.
Examine the challenges faced by women entrepreneurs in India and how government schemes can address them.
Suggest measures to further strengthen the ecosystem for women-led businesses in India.
articles.imageDescription: Diverse group of women entrepreneurs showcasing their products.
articles.trivia: India has seen a significant rise in women-led startups in recent years, contributing to economic dynamism.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Issues
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Role of women and women’s organization

articles.headline: BIMSTEC Foreign Ministers' Conclave Focuses on Regional Connectivity and Trade
articles.explainer:
• The Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC) Foreign Ministers' Conclave was held today, emphasizing enhanced regional connectivity and trade facilitation.
• Ministers discussed strategies to boost intra-BIMSTEC trade, investment, and tourism, leveraging the Bay of Bengal's strategic location.
• Key areas of cooperation included transport infrastructure development, digital connectivity, energy security, and combating transnational crimes.
• India reiterated its 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies, highlighting BIMSTEC as a crucial platform for regional prosperity and stability.
• The conclave also addressed common challenges like climate change and disaster management, promoting collective action among member states.
prelimsPoints: BIMSTEC: Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation.
Member states: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Thailand.
Focus on regional connectivity, trade, energy, disaster management.
Aligns with India's 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of BIMSTEC for India's regional foreign policy and economic integration.
Analyze the potential of BIMSTEC to foster greater connectivity and trade in the Bay of Bengal region.
Examine the challenges faced by BIMSTEC as a regional grouping and suggest measures for its revitalization.
Evaluate how BIMSTEC complements India's other regional and sub-regional initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of BIMSTEC member countries arranged in a circle.
articles.trivia: BIMSTEC was established in 1997 through the Bangkok Declaration.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Report Urges Accelerated Investment in Renewable Energy Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• A new report by NITI Aayog highlighted the critical need for accelerated investment in renewable energy infrastructure and grid modernization to meet India's ambitious climate targets.
• The report identified bottlenecks in land acquisition, transmission infrastructure, and financing as major hurdles to faster renewable energy deployment.
• It recommended policy interventions such as streamlined regulatory approvals, innovative financing mechanisms, and promotion of hybrid renewable energy projects.
• The report also underscored the importance of energy storage solutions and smart grid technologies for integrating a higher share of renewables into the national grid.
• NITI Aayog emphasized collaboration between central and state governments, as well as the private sector, to achieve energy transition goals.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is a policy think tank of the Government of India.
Report highlights challenges in renewable energy deployment (land, transmission, finance).
Recommends policy reforms, innovative financing, hybrid projects.
Emphasizes smart grids and energy storage for renewables integration.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze India's progress and challenges in achieving its renewable energy targets.
Discuss the key recommendations of the NITI Aayog report for accelerating renewable energy deployment.
Examine the role of policy, technology, and financing in India's energy transition journey.
Evaluate the significance of grid modernization and energy storage for a renewable-dominated power system.
articles.imageDescription: Solar panels and wind turbines against a backdrop of urban infrastructure.
articles.trivia: India aims to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.

articles.headline: New PLI Scheme Introduced for Advanced Materials Manufacturing Sector
articles.explainer:
• The government announced a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically tailored for the Advanced Materials Manufacturing sector, aiming to boost domestic production and reduce import dependence.
• The scheme offers incentives on incremental sales of goods manufactured in India, encouraging both domestic and foreign companies to invest in high-value-added advanced materials.
• This initiative is crucial for sectors like electronics, automotive, aerospace, and defence, which rely heavily on specialized materials.
• The PLI scheme is expected to foster innovation, create skilled jobs, and integrate India into global supply chains for advanced materials.
• It is part of the broader 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision, promoting self-reliance and enhancing India's manufacturing capabilities.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme provides incentives on incremental sales for domestic manufacturing.
New scheme targets Advanced Materials Manufacturing sector.
Aims to reduce import dependence in critical sectors.
Part of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and features of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for the Advanced Materials sector.
Analyze how such schemes contribute to India's manufacturing growth and integration into global supply chains.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in developing an indigenous advanced materials ecosystem in India.
Evaluate the role of government incentives in fostering innovation and creating skilled employment in high-tech manufacturing.
articles.imageDescription: Scientists working with advanced materials in a laboratory.
articles.trivia: PLI schemes have been introduced for 14 key sectors in India, including electronics, automobiles, pharmaceuticals, and textiles.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Develop Novel Therapeutic for Neglected Tropical Disease
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientists at a leading research institute have announced the development of a novel therapeutic compound showing high efficacy against a neglected tropical disease prevalent in several parts of the country.
• The breakthrough involved extensive research into the pathogen's biology and leveraging advanced drug discovery platforms.
• Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) disproportionately affect vulnerable populations and often lack adequate funding for research and treatment.
• This indigenous development is a significant step towards addressing public health challenges specific to India and other tropical regions.
• Clinical trials for the therapeutic are expected to commence soon, with hopes for rapid deployment to affected communities.
prelimsPoints: NTDs are a group of parasitic, bacterial, and viral diseases.
Disproportionately affect vulnerable populations.
Indigenous development of a therapeutic compound.
Clinical trials are the next step for drug development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the public health significance of Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) in India.
Analyze the role of indigenous research and development in addressing specific health challenges in developing countries.
Examine the challenges in drug discovery and development for NTDs and how they can be overcome.
Evaluate the potential impact of such scientific breakthroughs on healthcare access and affordability.
articles.imageDescription: Scientists in a laboratory setting, working with microscopes and test tubes.
articles.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) lists 20 diseases or groups of diseases as Neglected Tropical Diseases.

articles.headline: ASI Unearths Ancient Habitation Site in Haryana, Reveals Indus Valley Link
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of an ancient habitation site in Haryana, yielding significant artifacts that suggest a strong link to the late Harappan period or Indus Valley Civilization.
• Excavations have uncovered pottery, tools, seals, and structural remains, indicating a well-developed urban settlement.
• The findings provide new insights into the geographical spread and cultural practices of the Indus Valley Civilization, particularly in the Ghaggar-Hakra river basin.
• This discovery could potentially reshape understanding of the transition phases and regional variations within the Harappan civilization.
• Further research and carbon dating are underway to establish precise chronology and cultural affiliations of the site.
prelimsPoints: ASI is responsible for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
Indus Valley Civilization (Harappan Civilization) flourished around 2500-1900 BCE.
Ghaggar-Hakra river basin is a significant region for Harappan sites.
Artifacts like pottery, tools, seals provide cultural insights.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of new archaeological discoveries in understanding the Indus Valley Civilization and its geographical extent.
Analyze how recent findings contribute to our knowledge of Harappan urban planning, trade, and cultural practices.
Examine the role of archaeology in reconstructing ancient Indian history and preserving cultural heritage.
Evaluate the challenges and ethical considerations involved in archaeological excavations and interpretations.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating an ancient site, revealing pottery shards.
articles.trivia: Rakhigarhi in Haryana is one of the largest Harappan sites, older than Mohenjo-Daro.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Reforms for Civil Services Capacity Building
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted a report recommending comprehensive reforms in civil services capacity building, focusing on continuous learning, specialization, and performance-based promotions.
• The panel emphasized the need to adapt training modules to address emerging challenges like digital governance, climate change, and complex socio-economic issues.
• Recommendations included a structured mid-career training program, integration of technology in governance, and greater emphasis on ethical conduct and public service values.
• The report also suggested rationalizing the recruitment process and fostering a culture of innovation and accountability within the bureaucracy.
• These reforms aim to enhance the efficiency, responsiveness, and effectiveness of India's civil services in meeting the demands of modern governance.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committees examine bills and departmental work.
Civil services reforms aim to enhance efficiency and responsiveness.
Focus on continuous learning, specialization, ethical conduct.
Mission Karmayogi is a national programme for civil services capacity building.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of civil services capacity building in the context of India's development trajectory.
Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Panel for reforming civil services training and career progression.
Examine the challenges in implementing comprehensive civil services reforms and suggest strategies for overcoming them.
Evaluate how Mission Karmayogi aligns with the proposed reforms and its potential impact on governance.
articles.imageDescription: Conceptual image of civil servants undergoing training and interacting with technology.
articles.trivia: Mission Karmayogi, launched in 2020, aims to transform civil service capacity building through an online platform iGOT-Karmayogi.

articles.headline: G20 Sherpas Deliberate on Global Economic Challenges and Sustainable Development Goals
articles.explainer:
• G20 Sherpas convened for a crucial meeting to prepare the agenda for the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit, focusing on global economic stability, climate action, and the acceleration of Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
• Discussions revolved around addressing persistent inflation, geopolitical tensions impacting global supply chains, and debt vulnerabilities in developing nations.
• Sherpas also deliberated on strengthening multilateral cooperation for climate finance, technology transfer, and achieving net-zero emissions targets.
• India, as a prominent voice, emphasized inclusive growth, digital public infrastructure, and reforms in multilateral development banks.
• The meeting aimed to build consensus on key declarations and commitments to be adopted by the G20 leaders.
prelimsPoints: G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
Sherpas are personal representatives of the leaders of G20 countries.
Focus areas include global economic stability, climate action, SDGs.
India advocates for inclusive growth and digital public infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of the G20 in addressing contemporary global economic and developmental challenges.
Analyze India's contributions and priorities within the G20 framework, particularly concerning inclusive growth and digital public infrastructure.
Examine the challenges in achieving consensus among G20 members on issues like climate finance and debt relief.
Evaluate the effectiveness of multilateral forums like G20 in driving global cooperation for sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: G20 logo with flags of member countries.
articles.trivia: The first G20 Leaders' Summit was held in Washington D.C. in 2008.

articles.headline: Urban Local Bodies Mandated to Adopt Smart Waste Management Technologies
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs issued a directive mandating all urban local bodies (ULBs) to adopt smart waste management technologies and practices to enhance efficiency and sustainability.
• The directive promotes the use of IoT-enabled bins, GPS-tracked collection vehicles, waste-to-energy plants, and advanced recycling facilities.
• This move aims to address the growing challenge of urban waste generation, improve public health, and reduce environmental pollution.
• ULBs are encouraged to integrate these technologies with existing Swachh Bharat Mission initiatives and explore public-private partnerships.
• Capacity building for municipal staff and public awareness campaigns on source segregation are also key components of the new mandate.
prelimsPoints: ULBs are responsible for urban governance and services.
Smart waste management uses IoT, GPS, waste-to-energy plants.
Aims to improve public health and reduce pollution.
Integrates with Swachh Bharat Mission initiatives.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges of urban waste management in India and the need for technological interventions.
Analyze how smart waste management technologies can contribute to sustainable urban development and public health.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships and citizen participation in effective waste management.
Evaluate the progress and future potential of the Swachh Bharat Mission in transforming urban sanitation and waste management.
articles.imageDescription: Smart waste bins connected digitally in an urban setting.
articles.trivia: India generates over 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of municipal solid waste every day.

articles.headline: Government Approves New Solar Energy Park Development in Rajasthan
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) has approved the development of a new large-scale Solar Energy Park in Rajasthan, aiming to significantly boost the state's renewable energy capacity.
• The park, spread over several thousand acres, will attract substantial private investment in solar power generation and associated infrastructure.
• This initiative is part of India's broader strategy to achieve its ambitious renewable energy targets and reduce its carbon footprint.
• Rajasthan, with its abundant solar insolation, is a key state for solar energy development, already hosting several large solar parks.
• The project is expected to create local employment opportunities and contribute to the economic development of the region.
prelimsPoints: MNRE is the nodal ministry for renewable energy.
Rajasthan has high solar insolation, ideal for solar projects.
Solar parks facilitate infrastructure for multiple solar projects.
Aims to increase renewable energy capacity and reduce carbon footprint.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of solar energy parks in accelerating India's renewable energy transition.
Analyze the geographical and economic factors that make Rajasthan a leading state for solar power development.
Examine the challenges and benefits associated with large-scale renewable energy projects.
Evaluate the government's policies and incentives for promoting private investment in the renewable energy sector.
articles.imageDescription: Vast solar panel field under a clear sky in a desert landscape.
articles.trivia: Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan is one of the largest solar parks in the world.

articles.headline: Government Strengthens Cybersecurity Framework for Critical Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) announced enhanced measures to bolster India's cybersecurity framework, particularly for critical information infrastructure.
• The new guidelines mandate stricter compliance standards, regular security audits, and real-time threat intelligence sharing across vital sectors like finance, energy, transport, and defence.
• This move is a response to the increasing sophistication of cyber-attacks and the growing geopolitical risks associated with digital infrastructure.
• Emphasis is placed on indigenous cybersecurity solutions and capacity building among personnel managing critical systems.
• The framework also includes provisions for rapid incident response and recovery mechanisms to minimize disruption from cyber threats.
prelimsPoints: NCIIPC is the nodal agency for protecting critical information infrastructure.
Critical infrastructure includes finance, energy, transport, defence.
New measures include stricter compliance, audits, threat intelligence sharing.
Focus on indigenous solutions and capacity building.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threats to India's cybersecurity, especially concerning critical information infrastructure.
Analyze the key components of India's strengthened cybersecurity framework and its implications for national security.
Examine the challenges in securing critical infrastructure in the digital age and suggest strategies for effective protection.
Evaluate the role of indigenous cybersecurity solutions and international cooperation in enhancing India's cyber resilience.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract digital network lines representing cybersecurity, with a shield icon.
articles.trivia: India established CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) in 2004 as its national agency for cyber security incidents.

articles.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches New Initiative to Promote Heritage Tourism Circuits
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism unveiled a new initiative aimed at developing and promoting specialized heritage tourism circuits across India, focusing on cultural richness and historical significance.
• The initiative involves identifying lesser-known historical sites, improving connectivity, developing tourist amenities, and training local guides.
• It seeks to diversify India's tourism offerings beyond conventional destinations, creating immersive cultural experiences for both domestic and international visitors.
• The program will also emphasize sustainable tourism practices and community participation to ensure the preservation of heritage sites and economic benefits for local populations.
• Collaboration with state tourism boards and cultural organizations is central to the successful implementation of these heritage circuits.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Tourism promotes heritage tourism.
Focus on lesser-known sites, connectivity, amenities, local guides.
Aims to diversify tourism and promote sustainable practices.
Involves collaboration with state tourism boards.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of heritage tourism in India for economic development and cultural preservation.
Analyze the strategies adopted by the Ministry of Tourism to develop and promote specialized heritage circuits.
Examine the challenges in balancing tourism promotion with the conservation of historical sites and local cultures.
Evaluate the role of sustainable tourism practices and community involvement in enhancing heritage tourism experiences.
articles.imageDescription: A famous historical monument bathed in evening light, surrounded by tourists.
articles.trivia: India has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, covering both cultural and natural heritage.

highlights: Government unveils National Logistics Policy 2.0 to enhance India's trade competitiveness and reduce logistics costs to global benchmarks.
Supreme Court delivers a landmark judgment on inter-state water dispute resolution, emphasizing time-bound equitable apportionment.
India and the US strengthen strategic partnership, focusing on critical technologies, defence, and a free Indo-Pacific under the iCET framework.
ISRO successfully launches the advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-07', boosting remote sensing capabilities for various applications.
Winter Session of Parliament commences, with key legislative debates expected on the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill and Uniform Civil Code.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Logistics Policy (NLP) 2.0:
1. It aims to reduce logistics costs to approximately 8% of India's GDP.
2. It leverages the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan for integrated infrastructure development.
3. The policy primarily focuses on air cargo logistics and digital integration.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: NLP 2.0 aims to reduce logistics costs to approximately 8% of GDP. Statement 2 is correct: The policy leverages PM Gati Shakti. Statement 3 is incorrect: The policy focuses on integrated digital logistics systems and multimodal connectivity, not primarily air cargo alone, but all modes of transport.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to Inter-State Water Disputes in India, consider the following statements:
1. Article 262 of the Indian Constitution empowers Parliament to provide for the adjudication of any dispute or complaint with respect to the use, distribution, or control of the waters of any inter-State river or river valley.
2. The Supreme Court has original jurisdiction over inter-state water disputes and can directly hear and decide such cases.
3. The Inter-State Water Disputes Act, 1956, allows the Central Government to set up a tribunal for the adjudication of such disputes.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as per Article 262. Statement 2 is incorrect; Article 262 specifically excludes the Supreme Court's jurisdiction in such matters if Parliament so provides. Statement 3 is correct, the Act provides for the establishment of tribunals.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about India's National Green Hydrogen Mission is/are correct?
1. Its primary objective is to make India a global hub for the production and export of green hydrogen.
2. Green hydrogen is produced through the electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
3. The mission targets a production capacity of 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen per annum by 2030.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. Green hydrogen is indeed produced via electrolysis powered by renewable energy. India's target is 5 million tonnes per annum by 2030.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following applications of Earth Observation Satellites (EOS):
1. Agricultural crop monitoring and yield estimation.
2. Urban planning and infrastructure development.
3. Disaster management and early warning systems.
4. Climate change monitoring and environmental assessment.
Which of the above applications are correctly associated with Earth Observation Satellites?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Earth Observation Satellites provide imagery and data crucial for a wide range of applications, including all the listed points: agriculture, urban planning, disaster management, and environmental/climate monitoring. EOS-07 specifically covers many of these areas.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC), which of the following countries is NOT a member?
prelimsMocks.explanation: BIMSTEC comprises seven member states: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand. Maldives is not a member of BIMSTEC.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the potential impact of the National Logistics Policy 2.0 on India's economic growth and export competitiveness. What challenges might hinder its effective implementation, and how can they be addressed?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment. Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NLP 2.0 and its objectives. Detail its potential positive impacts on GDP, trade, and employment by reducing logistics costs and improving efficiency. Identify challenges such as infrastructure gaps, digital literacy, inter-agency coordination, and funding. Suggest measures like accelerated infrastructure development, skill enhancement, regulatory reforms, and public-private partnerships.

mainsMocks.question: Examine the evolving nature of India-US strategic partnership, particularly in the domains of critical technologies and defence. How does the iCET framework contribute to this collaboration, and what are the opportunities and challenges for India?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests. Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the historical trajectory and current significance of India-US ties. Focus on the deepening cooperation in critical technologies (AI, semiconductors, quantum) and defence (co-production, co-development). Explain iCET's role as a facilitator. Discuss opportunities like technology transfer, defense industrial base strengthening, and regional security. Highlight challenges such as technology denial regimes, intellectual property concerns, and balancing relations with other powers.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze India's progress and challenges in achieving its renewable energy targets. What policy interventions and technological advancements are necessary to accelerate the energy transition and ensure energy security?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment. Infrastructure: Energy.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's ambitious renewable energy targets and current progress. Analyze the challenges, including land acquisition, transmission infrastructure, financing gaps, intermittency, and grid integration. Discuss policy interventions like NITI Aayog's recommendations (streamlined approvals, innovative financing), and technological advancements (energy storage, smart grids, green hydrogen). Conclude with strategies for a holistic approach to energy security and sustainable transition.

quickBytes.text: India's logistics cost is estimated at 13-14% of GDP, higher than developed economies (8-10%).
Article 262 of the Indian Constitution deals with the adjudication of inter-state river water disputes.
iCET (Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies) is a key framework for India-US tech cooperation.
National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen production by 2030.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's workhorse rocket for launching Earth Observation Satellites.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises 6 members, 3 from RBI and 3 external members.
Article 44 of the Indian Constitution is a Directive Principle of State Policy (DPSP) related to Uniform Civil Code.
Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes offer incentives to boost domestic manufacturing in key sectors.
BIMSTEC was established in 1997 through the Bangkok Declaration, fostering regional cooperation.
NCIIPC (National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre) is India's nodal agency for critical infra cybersecurity.

GS1.headline: Ministry Launches 'Nari Shakti Udyam' Scheme for Women Entrepreneurs
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women & Child Development launched a new flagship scheme, 'Nari Shakti Udyam', aimed at fostering women entrepreneurship and promoting self-reliance among women across India.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, skill development training, mentorship, and market linkages to women aspiring to start or expand their businesses.
• It focuses on empowering women from marginalized communities and rural areas, ensuring inclusive economic growth.
• The initiative aligns with the government's broader goal of gender equality and economic empowerment of women.
• Partnerships with financial institutions, NGOs, and industry bodies are envisioned to ensure the successful implementation and outreach of the scheme.
prelimsPoints: Nari Shakti Udyam scheme focuses on women entrepreneurship.
Provides financial aid, training, mentorship, market linkages.
Aimed at women from marginalized and rural areas.
Part of broader gender equality and economic empowerment goals.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of women entrepreneurship in achieving inclusive economic growth and gender equality in India.
Analyze the key features and potential impact of the 'Nari Shakti Udyam' scheme on women's economic empowerment.
Examine the challenges faced by women entrepreneurs in India and how government schemes can address them.
Suggest measures to further strengthen the ecosystem for women-led businesses in India.
GS1.imageDescription: Diverse group of women entrepreneurs showcasing their products.
GS1.trivia: India has seen a significant rise in women-led startups in recent years, contributing to economic dynamism.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Issues
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Role of women and women’s organization

GS1.headline: ASI Unearths Ancient Habitation Site in Haryana, Reveals Indus Valley Link
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of an ancient habitation site in Haryana, yielding significant artifacts that suggest a strong link to the late Harappan period or Indus Valley Civilization.
• Excavations have uncovered pottery, tools, seals, and structural remains, indicating a well-developed urban settlement.
• The findings provide new insights into the geographical spread and cultural practices of the Indus Valley Civilization, particularly in the Ghaggar-Hakra river basin.
• This discovery could potentially reshape understanding of the transition phases and regional variations within the Harappan civilization.
• Further research and carbon dating are underway to establish precise chronology and cultural affiliations of the site.
prelimsPoints: ASI is responsible for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
Indus Valley Civilization (Harappan Civilization) flourished around 2500-1900 BCE.
Ghaggar-Hakra river basin is a significant region for Harappan sites.
Artifacts like pottery, tools, seals provide cultural insights.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of new archaeological discoveries in understanding the Indus Valley Civilization and its geographical extent.
Analyze how recent findings contribute to our knowledge of Harappan urban planning, trade, and cultural practices.
Examine the role of archaeology in reconstructing ancient Indian history and preserving cultural heritage.
Evaluate the challenges and ethical considerations involved in archaeological excavations and interpretations.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating an ancient site, revealing pottery shards.
GS1.trivia: Rakhigarhi in Haryana is one of the largest Harappan sites, older than Mohenjo-Daro.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches New Initiative to Promote Heritage Tourism Circuits
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism unveiled a new initiative aimed at developing and promoting specialized heritage tourism circuits across India, focusing on cultural richness and historical significance.
• The initiative involves identifying lesser-known historical sites, improving connectivity, developing tourist amenities, and training local guides.
• It seeks to diversify India's tourism offerings beyond conventional destinations, creating immersive cultural experiences for both domestic and international visitors.
• The program will also emphasize sustainable tourism practices and community participation to ensure the preservation of heritage sites and economic benefits for local populations.
• Collaboration with state tourism boards and cultural organizations is central to the successful implementation of these heritage circuits.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Tourism promotes heritage tourism.
Focus on lesser-known sites, connectivity, amenities, local guides.
Aims to diversify tourism and promote sustainable practices.
Involves collaboration with state tourism boards.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of heritage tourism in India for economic development and cultural preservation.
Analyze the strategies adopted by the Ministry of Tourism to develop and promote specialized heritage circuits.
Examine the challenges in balancing tourism promotion with the conservation of historical sites and local cultures.
Evaluate the role of sustainable tourism practices and community involvement in enhancing heritage tourism experiences.
GS1.imageDescription: A famous historical monument bathed in evening light, surrounded by tourists.
GS1.trivia: India has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, covering both cultural and natural heritage.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Delivers Landmark Verdict on Inter-State Water Dispute Resolution
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a significant ruling, clarified the jurisdiction and powers of the Inter-State Water Disputes Tribunals, emphasizing the need for time-bound resolution.
• The judgment reinforced the principle of equitable apportionment of water resources among riparian states, considering various factors like population, cultivable area, and existing uses.
• It called for greater parliamentary intervention to streamline the legal framework for water dispute resolution, suggesting amendments to the Inter-State Water Disputes Act, 1956.
• The verdict is expected to have far-reaching implications for ongoing and future water-sharing disputes, promoting cooperative federalism.
• The court also highlighted the importance of scientific data and expert opinions in tribunal proceedings.
prelimsPoints: Inter-State Water Disputes Act, 1956 empowers the Centre to set up tribunals.
Article 262 of the Constitution deals with adjudication of water disputes.
Supreme Court cannot ordinarily intervene in tribunal awards but can interpret laws.
Concept of equitable apportionment of water.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional provisions and legal framework for inter-state water dispute resolution in India.
Discuss the challenges in resolving inter-state water disputes and suggest reforms for effective resolution.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in promoting cooperative federalism through judgments on resource sharing.
Examine the socio-economic and political implications of protracted water disputes.
GS2.imageDescription: Symbolic image of water flowing between states, overseen by judicial scales.
GS2.trivia: The Ravi & Beas Waters Tribunal, set up in 1986, is one of the longest-running water tribunals in India.

GS2.headline: India, US Strengthen Strategic Partnership in Critical Technologies and Defence
GS2.explainer:
• The India-US Strategic Dialogue concluded with both nations committing to deepen cooperation in emerging and critical technologies, including AI, semiconductors, and quantum computing.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defence collaboration, co-production, and co-development of military platforms under the iCET (Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies) framework.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, agreeing to coordinate efforts on regional security challenges.
• Agreements were reached to boost bilateral trade and investment, with a focus on resilient supply chains and diversified manufacturing.
• The dialogue also covered cooperation on climate change, space exploration, and global health initiatives.
prelimsPoints: iCET: Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies.
India-US focus on AI, quantum, semiconductors, space, biotechnology.
Cooperation for free and open Indo-Pacific.
Bilateral trade and investment targets.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-US strategic partnership for regional and global stability.
Discuss the opportunities and challenges in enhancing defence and critical technology cooperation between India and the US.
Evaluate how the iCET framework can contribute to India's technological self-reliance.
Examine the role of India-US collaboration in shaping the future of the Indo-Pacific region.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the USA, with symbols of technology and defence superimposed.
GS2.trivia: The US is one of India's largest trading partners and a significant source of FDI.

GS2.headline: Winter Session of Parliament Commences: Data Protection Bill and UCC Debates Expected
GS2.explainer:
• The Winter Session of the Indian Parliament began today, with a packed legislative agenda including several key bills and discussions on pressing national issues.
• High on the agenda are the anticipated debates and potential passage of the updated Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2024, and preliminary discussions on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
• Opposition parties are expected to raise concerns over economic slowdown, unemployment, and issues related to federalism and Centre-state relations.
• The session will also address various socio-economic issues, including agricultural distress and women's safety.
• Several legislative proposals, including amendments to existing laws, are slated for consideration during the session.
prelimsPoints: Winter Session is one of the three annual parliamentary sessions.
Digital Personal Data Protection Bill aims to protect personal data of individuals.
Uniform Civil Code (UCC) is mentioned in Article 44 of DPSP.
Parliament's role in law-making and accountability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Winter Session of Parliament in India's democratic functioning.
Analyze the key provisions and potential impact of the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill on individual rights and the digital economy.
Examine the constitutional implications and socio-political debates surrounding the Uniform Civil Code.
Evaluate the role of parliamentary debates in addressing national issues and ensuring government accountability.
GS2.imageDescription: Parliament House building in New Delhi.
GS2.trivia: The first Lok Sabha session was held on 13 May 1952.

GS2.headline: BIMSTEC Foreign Ministers' Conclave Focuses on Regional Connectivity and Trade
GS2.explainer:
• The Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC) Foreign Ministers' Conclave was held today, emphasizing enhanced regional connectivity and trade facilitation.
• Ministers discussed strategies to boost intra-BIMSTEC trade, investment, and tourism, leveraging the Bay of Bengal's strategic location.
• Key areas of cooperation included transport infrastructure development, digital connectivity, energy security, and combating transnational crimes.
• India reiterated its 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies, highlighting BIMSTEC as a crucial platform for regional prosperity and stability.
• The conclave also addressed common challenges like climate change and disaster management, promoting collective action among member states.
prelimsPoints: BIMSTEC: Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation.
Member states: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Thailand.
Focus on regional connectivity, trade, energy, disaster management.
Aligns with India's 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of BIMSTEC for India's regional foreign policy and economic integration.
Analyze the potential of BIMSTEC to foster greater connectivity and trade in the Bay of Bengal region.
Examine the challenges faced by BIMSTEC as a regional grouping and suggest measures for its revitalization.
Evaluate how BIMSTEC complements India's other regional and sub-regional initiatives.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of BIMSTEC member countries arranged in a circle.
GS2.trivia: BIMSTEC was established in 1997 through the Bangkok Declaration.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Reforms for Civil Services Capacity Building
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted a report recommending comprehensive reforms in civil services capacity building, focusing on continuous learning, specialization, and performance-based promotions.
• The panel emphasized the need to adapt training modules to address emerging challenges like digital governance, climate change, and complex socio-economic issues.
• Recommendations included a structured mid-career training program, integration of technology in governance, and greater emphasis on ethical conduct and public service values.
• The report also suggested rationalizing the recruitment process and fostering a culture of innovation and accountability within the bureaucracy.
• These reforms aim to enhance the efficiency, responsiveness, and effectiveness of India's civil services in meeting the demands of modern governance.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committees examine bills and departmental work.
Civil services reforms aim to enhance efficiency and responsiveness.
Focus on continuous learning, specialization, ethical conduct.
Mission Karmayogi is a national programme for civil services capacity building.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of civil services capacity building in the context of India's development trajectory.
Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Panel for reforming civil services training and career progression.
Examine the challenges in implementing comprehensive civil services reforms and suggest strategies for overcoming them.
Evaluate how Mission Karmayogi aligns with the proposed reforms and its potential impact on governance.
GS2.imageDescription: Conceptual image of civil servants undergoing training and interacting with technology.
GS2.trivia: Mission Karmayogi, launched in 2020, aims to transform civil service capacity building through an online platform iGOT-Karmayogi.

GS2.headline: G20 Sherpas Deliberate on Global Economic Challenges and Sustainable Development Goals
GS2.explainer:
• G20 Sherpas convened for a crucial meeting to prepare the agenda for the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit, focusing on global economic stability, climate action, and the acceleration of Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
• Discussions revolved around addressing persistent inflation, geopolitical tensions impacting global supply chains, and debt vulnerabilities in developing nations.
• Sherpas also deliberated on strengthening multilateral cooperation for climate finance, technology transfer, and achieving net-zero emissions targets.
• India, as a prominent voice, emphasized inclusive growth, digital public infrastructure, and reforms in multilateral development banks.
• The meeting aimed to build consensus on key declarations and commitments to be adopted by the G20 leaders.
prelimsPoints: G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
Sherpas are personal representatives of the leaders of G20 countries.
Focus areas include global economic stability, climate action, SDGs.
India advocates for inclusive growth and digital public infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of the G20 in addressing contemporary global economic and developmental challenges.
Analyze India's contributions and priorities within the G20 framework, particularly concerning inclusive growth and digital public infrastructure.
Examine the challenges in achieving consensus among G20 members on issues like climate finance and debt relief.
Evaluate the effectiveness of multilateral forums like G20 in driving global cooperation for sustainable development.
GS2.imageDescription: G20 logo with flags of member countries.
GS2.trivia: The first G20 Leaders' Summit was held in Washington D.C. in 2008.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils National Logistics Policy 2.0 to Enhance Global Competitiveness
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government introduced the updated National Logistics Policy (NLP) 2.0, aiming to reduce logistics costs from the current 13-14% of GDP to a global benchmark of 8%.
• The policy focuses on integrated digital logistics systems, multimodal connectivity, and process re-engineering to improve efficiency and transparency.
• Key pillars include the development of a unified logistics interface platform, standardization of warehousing, and skill development in the logistics sector.
• It is expected to significantly boost India's export competitiveness, facilitate ease of doing business, and create substantial employment opportunities.
• The policy leverages the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan for integrated infrastructure planning and execution.
prelimsPoints: National Logistics Policy 2.0 aims to reduce logistics cost to ~8% of GDP.
Key components: Digital integration, multimodal transport, skill development, standardization.
Leverages PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan.
Focus on 'Ease of Logistics' (EOL) index improvement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of NLP 2.0 in enhancing India's manufacturing and export competitiveness.
Analyze how digital integration and multimodal infrastructure can transform India's logistics sector.
Examine the challenges in implementing the NLP 2.0 and potential solutions.
Evaluate its contribution to achieving the 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' objectives.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating multimodal logistics network with digital integration.
GS3.trivia: The first National Logistics Policy was launched in 2022, aiming to create a single window e-logistics market.

GS3.headline: India Achieves Significant Milestone in Green Hydrogen Production Capacity
GS3.explainer:
• India announced a major step forward in its National Green Hydrogen Mission, achieving a milestone in establishing large-scale green hydrogen production capacity.
• The achievement is attributed to rapid advancements in electrolyzer technology, supportive government policies, and increasing private sector investment in the sector.
• Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using renewable electricity, offering a clean fuel alternative for hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and fertilizers.
• This development positions India as a potential global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• The government reiterated its commitment to further incentivize research and development, and infrastructure development for hydrogen storage and transportation.
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen produced via electrolysis using renewable energy.
National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen.
Applications in steel, cement, fertilizers, mobility.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate commitments.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private investment in accelerating India's green hydrogen mission.
Examine the potential socio-economic benefits of becoming a global green hydrogen hub.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-07'
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-07' onboard the PSLV-C58 rocket from Sriharikota.
• EOS-07 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications including agriculture, forestry, urban planning, disaster management, and coastal monitoring.
• The satellite carries advanced optical payloads and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) for all-weather, all-time imaging capabilities.
• This mission further strengthens India's indigenous remote sensing capabilities and contributes to global data sharing for environmental studies.
• The launch also included several co-passenger satellites developed by Indian startups and international clients, showcasing India's growing role in the global space economy.
prelimsPoints: EOS-07 is an Earth Observation Satellite.
Launched by PSLV-C58 rocket.
Applications: agriculture, forestry, disaster management, urban planning.
Carries advanced optical payloads and SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's socio-economic development and national security.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in remote sensing technology contribute to sustainable resource management and disaster mitigation.
Examine India's growing role in the global space economy, particularly through satellite launches for international clients and support for startups.
Evaluate the future trajectory of India's space program, focusing on its strategic and commercial aspects.
GS3.imageDescription: ISRO's PSLV rocket lifting off with the EOS-07 satellite.
GS3.trivia: PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle and has successfully launched hundreds of satellites.

GS3.headline: RBI Holds Key Policy Rates Steady Amidst Moderating Inflation Trends
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% in its latest review, maintaining its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• The decision was influenced by moderating retail inflation, which has shown signs of easing, though food price volatility remains a concern.
• The MPC emphasized its commitment to bringing inflation within the target band of 2-6% while supporting economic growth.
• RBI projected a stable GDP growth outlook for the current fiscal year, citing resilient domestic demand and robust investment.
• Experts suggest that the RBI is adopting a cautious approach, balancing inflation control with the need to sustain economic momentum.
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the policy interest rates.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a +/- 2% band.
Withdrawal of accommodation stance means reducing liquidity from the system.
mainsPoints: Discuss the current stance and rationale behind the RBI's monetary policy decisions.
Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various monetary policy tools in managing liquidity and credit conditions.
Examine the impact of global economic developments on India's monetary policy decisions.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo with graphs showing economic trends.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members, three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.

GS3.headline: Border Security Forces Fortify Defences Against Cross-Border Drone Incursions
GS3.explainer:
• Indian border security forces, particularly along the Western borders, have intensified surveillance and deployed advanced anti-drone technologies to counter increasing cross-border drone incursions.
• The drones are frequently used for smuggling narcotics, weapons, and dropping off surveillance equipment, posing a significant challenge to national security.
• The enhanced measures include the use of jammers, drone detection radars, and specialized counter-drone firearms and systems.
• This move comes after a series of incidents highlighting the evolving nature of hybrid warfare and the need for robust border management strategies.
• Training programs for personnel are also being ramped up to effectively operate new technologies and respond to drone threats.
prelimsPoints: BSF is India's primary border guarding force for land borders.
Drones used for smuggling narcotics, weapons, surveillance.
Counter-drone measures include jammers, radars, specialized firearms.
Threats from drones represent a challenge in hybrid warfare.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving nature of border security challenges posed by drone technology in India.
Discuss the technological and strategic measures adopted by India to counter cross-border drone incursions.
Examine the implications of drone proliferation for India's internal security and border management.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for enhancing India's anti-drone capabilities and intelligence gathering.
GS3.imageDescription: Border Security Force personnel operating anti-drone equipment.
GS3.trivia: India is developing indigenous counter-drone systems under the 'Make in India' initiative.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Report Urges Accelerated Investment in Renewable Energy Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by NITI Aayog highlighted the critical need for accelerated investment in renewable energy infrastructure and grid modernization to meet India's ambitious climate targets.
• The report identified bottlenecks in land acquisition, transmission infrastructure, and financing as major hurdles to faster renewable energy deployment.
• It recommended policy interventions such as streamlined regulatory approvals, innovative financing mechanisms, and promotion of hybrid renewable energy projects.
• The report also underscored the importance of energy storage solutions and smart grid technologies for integrating a higher share of renewables into the national grid.
• NITI Aayog emphasized collaboration between central and state governments, as well as the private sector, to achieve energy transition goals.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is a policy think tank of the Government of India.
Report highlights challenges in renewable energy deployment (land, transmission, finance).
Recommends policy reforms, innovative financing, hybrid projects.
Emphasizes smart grids and energy storage for renewables integration.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze India's progress and challenges in achieving its renewable energy targets.
Discuss the key recommendations of the NITI Aayog report for accelerating renewable energy deployment.
Examine the role of policy, technology, and financing in India's energy transition journey.
Evaluate the significance of grid modernization and energy storage for a renewable-dominated power system.
GS3.imageDescription: Solar panels and wind turbines against a backdrop of urban infrastructure.
GS3.trivia: India aims to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.

GS3.headline: New PLI Scheme Introduced for Advanced Materials Manufacturing Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The government announced a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically tailored for the Advanced Materials Manufacturing sector, aiming to boost domestic production and reduce import dependence.
• The scheme offers incentives on incremental sales of goods manufactured in India, encouraging both domestic and foreign companies to invest in high-value-added advanced materials.
• This initiative is crucial for sectors like electronics, automotive, aerospace, and defence, which rely heavily on specialized materials.
• The PLI scheme is expected to foster innovation, create skilled jobs, and integrate India into global supply chains for advanced materials.
• It is part of the broader 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision, promoting self-reliance and enhancing India's manufacturing capabilities.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme provides incentives on incremental sales for domestic manufacturing.
New scheme targets Advanced Materials Manufacturing sector.
Aims to reduce import dependence in critical sectors.
Part of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and features of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for the Advanced Materials sector.
Analyze how such schemes contribute to India's manufacturing growth and integration into global supply chains.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in developing an indigenous advanced materials ecosystem in India.
Evaluate the role of government incentives in fostering innovation and creating skilled employment in high-tech manufacturing.
GS3.imageDescription: Scientists working with advanced materials in a laboratory.
GS3.trivia: PLI schemes have been introduced for 14 key sectors in India, including electronics, automobiles, pharmaceuticals, and textiles.

GS3.headline: Indian Scientists Develop Novel Therapeutic for Neglected Tropical Disease
GS3.explainer:
• Indian scientists at a leading research institute have announced the development of a novel therapeutic compound showing high efficacy against a neglected tropical disease prevalent in several parts of the country.
• The breakthrough involved extensive research into the pathogen's biology and leveraging advanced drug discovery platforms.
• Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) disproportionately affect vulnerable populations and often lack adequate funding for research and treatment.
• This indigenous development is a significant step towards addressing public health challenges specific to India and other tropical regions.
• Clinical trials for the therapeutic are expected to commence soon, with hopes for rapid deployment to affected communities.
prelimsPoints: NTDs are a group of parasitic, bacterial, and viral diseases.
Disproportionately affect vulnerable populations.
Indigenous development of a therapeutic compound.
Clinical trials are the next step for drug development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the public health significance of Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) in India.
Analyze the role of indigenous research and development in addressing specific health challenges in developing countries.
Examine the challenges in drug discovery and development for NTDs and how they can be overcome.
Evaluate the potential impact of such scientific breakthroughs on healthcare access and affordability.
GS3.imageDescription: Scientists in a laboratory setting, working with microscopes and test tubes.
GS3.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) lists 20 diseases or groups of diseases as Neglected Tropical Diseases.

GS3.headline: Urban Local Bodies Mandated to Adopt Smart Waste Management Technologies
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs issued a directive mandating all urban local bodies (ULBs) to adopt smart waste management technologies and practices to enhance efficiency and sustainability.
• The directive promotes the use of IoT-enabled bins, GPS-tracked collection vehicles, waste-to-energy plants, and advanced recycling facilities.
• This move aims to address the growing challenge of urban waste generation, improve public health, and reduce environmental pollution.
• ULBs are encouraged to integrate these technologies with existing Swachh Bharat Mission initiatives and explore public-private partnerships.
• Capacity building for municipal staff and public awareness campaigns on source segregation are also key components of the new mandate.
prelimsPoints: ULBs are responsible for urban governance and services.
Smart waste management uses IoT, GPS, waste-to-energy plants.
Aims to improve public health and reduce pollution.
Integrates with Swachh Bharat Mission initiatives.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges of urban waste management in India and the need for technological interventions.
Analyze how smart waste management technologies can contribute to sustainable urban development and public health.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships and citizen participation in effective waste management.
Evaluate the progress and future potential of the Swachh Bharat Mission in transforming urban sanitation and waste management.
GS3.imageDescription: Smart waste bins connected digitally in an urban setting.
GS3.trivia: India generates over 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of municipal solid waste every day.

GS3.headline: Government Approves New Solar Energy Park Development in Rajasthan
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) has approved the development of a new large-scale Solar Energy Park in Rajasthan, aiming to significantly boost the state's renewable energy capacity.
• The park, spread over several thousand acres, will attract substantial private investment in solar power generation and associated infrastructure.
• This initiative is part of India's broader strategy to achieve its ambitious renewable energy targets and reduce its carbon footprint.
• Rajasthan, with its abundant solar insolation, is a key state for solar energy development, already hosting several large solar parks.
• The project is expected to create local employment opportunities and contribute to the economic development of the region.
prelimsPoints: MNRE is the nodal ministry for renewable energy.
Rajasthan has high solar insolation, ideal for solar projects.
Solar parks facilitate infrastructure for multiple solar projects.
Aims to increase renewable energy capacity and reduce carbon footprint.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of solar energy parks in accelerating India's renewable energy transition.
Analyze the geographical and economic factors that make Rajasthan a leading state for solar power development.
Examine the challenges and benefits associated with large-scale renewable energy projects.
Evaluate the government's policies and incentives for promoting private investment in the renewable energy sector.
GS3.imageDescription: Vast solar panel field under a clear sky in a desert landscape.
GS3.trivia: Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan is one of the largest solar parks in the world.

GS3.headline: Government Strengthens Cybersecurity Framework for Critical Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) announced enhanced measures to bolster India's cybersecurity framework, particularly for critical information infrastructure.
• The new guidelines mandate stricter compliance standards, regular security audits, and real-time threat intelligence sharing across vital sectors like finance, energy, transport, and defence.
• This move is a response to the increasing sophistication of cyber-attacks and the growing geopolitical risks associated with digital infrastructure.
• Emphasis is placed on indigenous cybersecurity solutions and capacity building among personnel managing critical systems.
• The framework also includes provisions for rapid incident response and recovery mechanisms to minimize disruption from cyber threats.
prelimsPoints: NCIIPC is the nodal agency for protecting critical information infrastructure.
Critical infrastructure includes finance, energy, transport, defence.
New measures include stricter compliance, audits, threat intelligence sharing.
Focus on indigenous solutions and capacity building.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threats to India's cybersecurity, especially concerning critical information infrastructure.
Analyze the key components of India's strengthened cybersecurity framework and its implications for national security.
Examine the challenges in securing critical infrastructure in the digital age and suggest strategies for effective protection.
Evaluate the role of indigenous cybersecurity solutions and international cooperation in enhancing India's cyber resilience.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract digital network lines representing cybersecurity, with a shield icon.
GS3.trivia: India established CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) in 2004 as its national agency for cyber security incidents.
